question: [File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: repay / onRepay callback] Can an unprivileged taker-as-payer, acting as the IRepayCallback contract, under preconditions where position[id][onBehalf].debt and marketState[id].withdrawable are both updated before the callback fires (Midnight.sol:508-509), trigger the sequence repay() -> onRepay() -> withdraw() to drain the newly-withdrawable units before safeTransferFrom pulls the loan token repayment (Midnight.sol:520), violating the invariant that withdrawable assets must be backed by received loan tokens, causing scoped impact: direct theft of lender-withdrawable assets with zero net loan token deposit? Proof idea: deploy a malicious IRepayCallback that reenters withdraw() during onRepay and assert that contract loan token balance decreases while withdrawable accounting goes negative.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / onBuy callback / withdraw] Can an unprivileged buyer-callback contract, under preconditions where buyerPos.credit and marketState.totalUnits are updated and EventsLib.Take is emitted before IBuyCallback.onBuy fires (Midnight.sol:425-452), trigger the sequence take(buy offer) -> onBuy() -> withdraw(onBehalf=buyer) to withdraw the freshly-credited units before safeTransferFrom pulls buyerAssets from the payer (Midnight.sol:455), violating the invariant that credit withdrawals must be backed by settled asset transfers, causing scoped impact: lender credit extracted without corresponding loan token inflow? Proof idea: fuzz units and buyerAssets; assert IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(midnight) does not decrease by more than pre-existing withdrawable after the full take sequence.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / onSell callback / repay] Can an unprivileged seller-callback contract, under preconditions where sellerPos.debt is increased and the liquidation lock is set via tExchange (Midnight.sol:444) before ISellCallback.onSell fires (Midnight.sol:458-474), trigger the sequence take(sell offer) -> onSell() -> repay(onBehalf=seller) to reduce seller debt before the post-callback health check at Midnight.sol:476, violating the invariant that the health check must reflect the true debt obligation created by the take, causing scoped impact: seller borrows assets while appearing healthy due to callback-assisted debt reduction? Proof idea: unit test where onSell reenters repay with units == sellerDebtIncrease and assert SellerIsLiquidatable is not incorrectly suppressed.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: liquidate / onLiquidate callback / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator-callback contract, under preconditions where _position.collateral[collateralIndex] is reduced and safeTransfer sends seized collateral before ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate fires (Midnight.sol:696-714), trigger the sequence liquidate() -> onLiquidate() -> liquidate() on the same borrower to seize collateral a second time from a position whose collateral was already zeroed, violating the invariant that seized collateral cannot exceed the borrower's actual deposited collateral, causing scoped impact: double-seizure of collateral or accounting underflow in _position.collateral? Proof idea: stateful fuzz test asserting sum(seized collateral across all liquidate calls) <= initial _position.collateral[collateralIndex].

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: liquidate / onLiquidate callback / withdraw] Can an unprivileged liquidator-callback contract, under preconditions where marketState[id].withdrawable is increased by repaidUnits before onLiquidate fires (Midnight.sol:675), trigger the sequence liquidate() -> onLiquidate() -> withdraw() to drain the newly-withdrawable repaid units before safeTransferFrom pulls the loan token repayment from the payer (Midnight.sol:717), violating the invariant that withdrawable must be backed by received loan tokens, causing scoped impact: lenders can withdraw loan tokens that the liquidator has not yet deposited? Proof idea: deploy malicious ILiquidateCallback that reenters withdraw() and assert IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(midnight) conservation.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / onBuy callback / take] Can an unprivileged buyer-callback contract, under preconditions where consumed[offer.maker][offer.group] is incremented before onBuy fires (Midnight.sol:368-373), trigger the sequence take(offer A) -> onBuy() -> take(offer B in same group) to consume the group budget twice within a single transaction, violating the invariant that the OCO group budget cannot be exceeded across offers, causing scoped impact: maker's capital is over-committed beyond maxAssets or maxUnits? Proof idea: fuzz two offers sharing a group; assert consumed[maker][group] <= offer.maxAssets after both takes complete.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / onSell callback / withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged seller-callback contract, under preconditions where sellerPos.debt is increased but the health check only runs after onSell returns (Midnight.sol:476), trigger the sequence take(sell offer) -> onSell() -> withdrawCollateral() to remove collateral during the callback window before the health check executes, violating the invariant that the seller must be healthy after take, causing scoped impact: seller borrows loan tokens while holding insufficient collateral, creating immediate bad debt? Proof idea: unit test where onSell reenters withdrawCollateral with all collateral and assert SellerIsLiquidatable reverts or health check catches the state.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: flashLoan / onFlashLoan callback / repay + withdraw] Can an unprivileged flash loan receiver, under preconditions where safeTransfer sends all requested tokens before onFlashLoan fires (Midnight.sol:742-748), trigger the sequence flashLoan() -> onFlashLoan() -> repay() -> withdraw() to repay a debt position and immediately withdraw the resulting credit within the flash loan callback, violating the invariant that flash loan repayment must restore the full token balance, causing scoped impact: protocol token balance deficit if withdraw drains more than the flash loan amount? Proof idea: invariant test asserting IERC20(token).balanceOf(midnight) after flashLoan >= before flashLoan for all token combinations.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / onBuy callback / liquidate] Can an unprivileged buyer-callback contract, under preconditions where the seller's liquidation lock is set via tExchange (Midnight.sol:444) but the buyer's position is not locked, trigger the sequence take(buy offer) -> onBuy() -> liquidate(borrower=buyer) to liquidate the buyer's pre-existing debt position during the callback window, violating the invariant that liquidation lock only protects the seller, causing scoped impact: buyer's collateral is seized mid-take while buyer's credit is simultaneously being increased? Proof idea: unit test with buyer having existing debt near liquidation threshold; assert buyer collateral is not seized during onBuy.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / onSell callback / take (nested)] Can an unprivileged seller-callback contract, under preconditions where the liquidation lock uses transient storage keyed by (id, seller) (UtilsLib.tExchange, Midnight.sol:444), trigger the sequence take(sell offer, seller=A) -> onSell() -> take(sell offer, seller=A, different market id) to bypass the liquidation lock for a different market, violating the invariant that the seller cannot be liquidated during any active take callback, causing scoped impact: seller A is liquidated in market B while their debt in market A is being increased? Proof idea: two-market fuzz test asserting liquidationLocked(id2, A) is false during onSell of market id1.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: repay / onRepay callback / liquidate] Can an unprivileged repay-callback contract, under preconditions where position[id][onBehalf].debt is reduced before onRepay fires (Midnight.sol:508), trigger the sequence repay(units=X) -> onRepay() -> liquidate(borrower=onBehalf) to attempt liquidation of a borrower whose debt was just reduced to zero, violating the invariant that NotBorrower() should revert when debt==0, causing scoped impact: if the revert is bypassed, bad debt realization runs on a zero-debt position corrupting lossFactor and totalUnits? Proof idea: unit test asserting liquidate reverts with NotBorrower when debt is zeroed by repay before callback completes.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / payer resolution / onBuy callback] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where payer is set to buyerCallback when buyerCallback != address(0) (Midnight.sol:422), trigger the sequence take(buy offer with callback) -> onBuy() -> setIsAuthorized(authorized=attacker, onBehalf=payer) to grant the attacker authorization over the callback-as-payer contract, violating the invariant that authorization changes must only be made by the account or its existing authorized operators, causing scoped impact: attacker gains full control over the callback contract's Midnight position? Proof idea: unit test asserting isAuthorized[callback][attacker] cannot be set to true by the callback itself during onBuy.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: claimContinuousFee / safeTransfer] Can an unprivileged fee claimer (feeClaimer role), under preconditions where continuousFeeCredit, totalUnits, and withdrawable are all decremented before safeTransfer fires (Midnight.sol:318-324), trigger the sequence claimContinuousFee() -> ERC777/callback token hook -> withdraw() to drain withdrawable units that were just decremented, violating the invariant that withdrawable must remain >= 0 and backed by actual token balance, causing scoped impact: accounting underflow in withdrawable or double-spend of withdrawable units? Proof idea: deploy ERC777 loan token whose tokensReceived hook reenters withdraw(); assert withdrawable never underflows.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / onSell callback / setConsumed] Can an unprivileged seller-callback contract, under preconditions where consumed[offer.maker][offer.group] is already incremented (Midnight.sol:368-373) before onSell fires, trigger the sequence take(sell offer) -> onSell() -> setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, onBehalf=maker) to cancel the maker's remaining group budget mid-take, violating the invariant that offer consumption state must be consistent before and after a take, causing scoped impact: maker's other OCO offers in the same group are permanently cancelled by the taker's callback? Proof idea: unit test asserting setConsumed cannot be called on behalf of maker by an unauthorized callback contract.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / EventsLib.Take emission ordering] Can an unprivileged off-chain indexer or on-chain contract, under preconditions where EventsLib.Take is emitted (Midnight.sol:425-442) before safeTransferFrom pulls funds (Midnight.sol:455-456), observe the Take event and trigger a reentrant take() on the same offer before the first take's safeTransferFrom executes, violating the invariant that consumed state must prevent double-fills, causing scoped impact: offer overfill if consumed check passes for both concurrent takes before either updates storage? Proof idea: differential test comparing consumed[maker][group] after sequential vs. callback-reentrant double-take.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: liquidate / bad debt realization / onLiquidate callback / updatePosition] Can an unprivileged liquidator-callback contract, under preconditions where lossFactor and totalUnits are updated during bad debt realization (Midnight.sol:631-634) before onLiquidate fires, trigger the sequence liquidate() -> onLiquidate() -> updatePosition(market, lender) to apply the new lossFactor to a lender's credit during the callback, violating the invariant that bad debt loss should be applied exactly once per lender interaction, causing scoped impact: lender credit is slashed during the liquidation callback and then slashed again at their next interaction? Proof idea: stateful fuzz asserting lender credit after two updatePosition calls equals credit after one call when lossFactor is unchanged.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / onBuy callback / supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged buyer-callback contract, under preconditions where buyerPos.debt is reduced before onBuy fires (Midnight.sol:408), trigger the sequence take(buy offer to reduce debt) -> onBuy() -> supplyCollateral() to supply collateral for the buyer during the callback, violating the invariant that collateral bitmap changes during a take callback should not affect the seller's health check, causing scoped impact: collateral bitmap corruption if supplyCollateral activates a new collateral index that changes the buyer's health evaluation? Proof idea: unit test asserting collateralBitmap state is consistent before and after onBuy callback.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / receiver and payer confusion] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where receiver is set to offer.receiverIfMakerIsSeller for buy offers (Midnight.sol:423) and payer is the buyerCallback, trigger the sequence take(buy offer, takerCallback=maliciousContract) -> onBuy() -> withdraw(receiver=attacker) to redirect the seller's asset payment to an attacker-controlled address, violating the invariant that sellerAssets must reach the intended receiver, causing scoped impact: maker's sale proceeds are stolen by the taker's callback? Proof idea: unit test asserting safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) sends to the correct receiver regardless of callback behavior.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: repay / onRepay callback / take] Can an unprivileged repay-callback contract, under preconditions where position[id][onBehalf].debt is zeroed before onRepay fires (Midnight.sol:508), trigger the sequence repay(all debt) -> onRepay() -> take(sell offer, seller=onBehalf) to create new debt for the borrower during the callback, violating the invariant that repay should atomically clear debt without allowing new debt creation in the same callback, causing scoped impact: borrower's debt is re-created after repay accounting but before the repay's safeTransferFrom pulls tokens, leaving the protocol with uncollected loan tokens? Proof idea: unit test asserting position[id][onBehalf].debt == 0 after repay completes even if onRepay reenters take.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / _updatePosition inside take / onBuy callback] Can an unprivileged buyer-callback contract, under preconditions where _updatePosition is called for buyer before onBuy fires (Midnight.sol:379) updating lastLossFactor and lastAccrual, trigger the sequence take() -> _updatePosition(buyer) -> onBuy() -> _updatePosition(buyer) via updatePosition() to apply fee accrual twice in the same block, violating the invariant that continuous fee accrual must be idempotent within the same timestamp, causing scoped impact: double-accrual of continuousFeeCredit or double-slash of buyer credit? Proof idea: unit test asserting updatePosition called twice in same block produces identical credit as calling once.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: liquidate / postMaturityMode / onLiquidate callback / take] Can an unprivileged liquidator-callback contract, under preconditions where postMaturityMode liquidation does not check the enter gate (Midnight.sol:597-600 only checks liquidatorGate), trigger the sequence liquidate(postMaturityMode=true) -> onLiquidate() -> take(sell offer, seller=borrower) to increase the borrower's debt post-maturity during the liquidation callback, violating the invariant that debt cannot increase after maturity (Midnight.sol:391), causing scoped impact: borrower accumulates new debt during liquidation callback, corrupting repaidUnits accounting? Proof idea: unit test asserting CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity reverts any take that increases seller debt when block.timestamp > market.maturity.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / tExchange liquidation lock / nested take same seller] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where tExchange returns wasLocked=false for the first take and sets the lock (Midnight.sol:444), trigger the sequence take(sell offer, seller=A) -> onSell() -> take(sell offer, seller=A, same market) to nest a second take for the same seller within the first take's callback, violating the invariant that the seller cannot be liquidated during take callbacks, causing scoped impact: the inner take's health check at Midnight.sol:476 runs while the outer take's debt increase is not yet finalized, potentially allowing an unhealthy combined position? Proof idea: fuzz nested takes for same seller and assert isHealthy(market, id, seller) holds after both takes complete.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / onBuy callback / claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged buyer-callback contract (if feeClaimer role is held by a contract), under preconditions where marketState.continuousFeeCredit is not yet updated during take, trigger the sequence take() -> onBuy() -> claimContinuousFee(amount > continuousFeeCredit) to claim more continuous fee credit than exists, violating the invariant that continuousFeeCredit must not underflow, causing scoped impact: arithmetic underflow in continuousFeeCredit or totalUnits leading to accounting corruption? Proof idea: unit test asserting claimContinuousFee reverts or continuousFeeCredit never underflows when called during onBuy.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: take / sellerCallback / isHealthy post-callback] Can an unprivileged seller-callback contract, under preconditions where the health check at Midnight.sol:476 uses the current oracle price at callback-return time, trigger the sequence take(sell offer) -> onSell() -> [oracle price manipulation via another protocol] to make the seller appear healthy at health check time despite being undercollateralized at take time, violating the invariant that the seller must be healthy based on the state created by the take, causing scoped impact: seller borrows at a price that makes them immediately unhealthy after the callback window closes? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price changes between take execution and health check; assert health check uses a price that cannot be manipulated by the callback.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: multicall / take + withdraw in same multicall] Can an unprivileged lender, under preconditions where multicall uses delegatecall (Midnight.sol:213) and msg.sender is preserved, trigger the sequence multicall([take(buy offer), withdraw(units=creditJustReceived)]) to withdraw credit in the same transaction as it is created, violating the invariant that withdrawable must be backed by settled repayments or prior lender deposits, causing scoped impact: lender withdraws loan tokens that were never deposited into the protocol? Proof idea: invariant test asserting IERC20(loanToken).balanceOf(midnight) >= withdrawable after any multicall sequence.
